page: Hetzner Cloud cpx11 is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory, which equates to approximately 0.98 GB per core. The CPU configuration includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 32 KB of L1d cache, and 0.5 MB of L2 cache. The instance is provisioned with 40 GB of local SSD storage and does not include any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the cpx11 in the lower performance tiers. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests rank in the bottom 10%, while PassMark CPU and memory scores fall into the weak and poor categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with read-only large block speeds at 31.66 GB/sec and write speeds at 18.69 GB/sec. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for small LLM models achieve average tier results, with the latter reaching 120.03 tokens/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, the cpx11 represents a highly budget-oriented hardware profile. It offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high memory density. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU model and limited memory bandwidth, which makes it unsuitable for sustained high-load compute tasks or memory-intensive applications.

This server is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, small-scale development or testing environments, simple automation scripts, and low-traffic applications that can tolerate shared resource contention and lower overall throughput.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx11 is an entry-level virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 2.0 GB of RAM. It includes 40 GB of local SSD storage but lacks dedicated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks indicate that the cpx11 generally operates in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, it achieves average performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for small LLM models. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low resource density, this server is a highly cost-effective option for budget-conscious, low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight development environments, and simple background tasks.
